message.content:
Question: When invoking Claude Code from the command line non-interactively (e.g., `claude -p "prompt"` or similar), is there a flag or setting that controls "effort level" / thinking budget / extended thinking depth for the response?

I'm specifically interested in:
1. Any CLI flag like `--thinking`, `--effort`, `--budget`, `--max-thinking-tokens`, etc.
2. Any environment variable that sets thinking budget
3. Any way via settings.json that affects how much "effort" or thinking the model applies to a one-shot prompt invocation

Report what's actually documented/available. Under 150 words.

content.text: I'll check the Claude Code documentation for CLI flags and settings related to thinking/effort control.

input.prompt: CLI flags for thinking, effort, budget, extended thinking, max tokens control

content.content:
# Claude Code Documentation Overview

This is the official documentation for **Claude Code**, Anthropic's agentic coding tool available across multiple platforms (terminal, IDE, desktop, browser).

## Key Documentation Areas

### Getting Started
- **[Quickstart](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart.md)** - Welcome and initial setup
- **[Desktop Quickstart](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop-quickstart.md)** - Install and run the desktop app
- **[Web Quickstart](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/web-quickstart.md)** - Run Claude Code in your browser

### Core Concepts
- **[How Claude Code Works](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works.md)** - Agentic loop, built-in tools, and interactions
- **[Glossary](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/glossary.md)** - Key terminology (agentic loop, compaction, CLAUDE.md, hooks, MCP, etc.)
- **[Overview](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview.md)** - High-level introduction

### Agent SDK (Programmatic Usage)
- **[Agent SDK Overview](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview.md)** - Build production AI agents with Claude Code as a library
- **[Python Reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/python.md)** and **[TypeScript Reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript.md)**
- **[Agent Loop](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/agent-loop.md)** - Message lifecycle and architecture

### Configuration & Customization
- **[CLAUDE.md & Memory](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.md)** - Persistent project instructions
- **[Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills.md)** - Extend capabilities
- **[Hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md)** - Intercept and control behavior
- **[MCP Integration](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.md)** - Connect external tools via Model Context Protocol
- **[Plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins.md)** - Extend Claude Code with custom extensions

### Enterprise & Deployment
- **[Admin Setup](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/admin-setup.md)** - Organization-wide deployment
- **[Secure Deployment](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/secure-deployment.md)** - Security best practices
- **[Server-Managed Settings](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/server-managed-settings.md)** - Centralized configuration

### Platforms
- VS Code, JetBrains IDEs, Desktop App, Web, CLI, GitHub Enterprise, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and more

## Common Tasks
- **[Common Workflows](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows.md)** - Exploring codebases, fixing bugs, refactoring, testing
- **[Best Practices](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices.md)** - Tips for optimal usage
- **[Troubleshooting](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting.md)** - Fix common issues

content.text: Let me check the settings documentation:

input.prompt: thinking effort budget tokens alwaysThinking effortLevel

content.content:
# Claude Code Settings Configuration

Claude Code offers a comprehensive settings system organized by **scope** to control behavior across different contexts.

## Configuration Scopes

| Scope | Location | Who It Affects | Shared? |
|-------|----------|----------------|---------|
| **Managed** | Server/plist/registry/system | All machine users | Yes (IT-deployed) |
| **User** | `~/.claude/` | You, all projects | No |
| **Project** | `.claude/` in repo | All collaborators | Yes (git-committed) |
| **Local** | `.claude/settings.local.json` | You, this repo only | No (gitignored) |

**Precedence** (highest to lowest):
1. Managed (can't be overridden)
2. Command-line arguments
3. Local settings
4. Project settings
5. User settings

## Settings Files

- **`~/.claude/settings.json`** - User settings (all projects)
- **`.claude/settings.json`** - Project settings (shared with team)
- **`.claude/settings.local.json`** - Personal overrides (not shared)
- **`~/.claude.json`** - OAuth, MCP servers, caches, trust settings

## Key Configuration Areas

### Permissions
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run lint)", "Read(~/.zshrc)"],
    "deny": ["Bash(curl *)", "Read(./.env)"],
    "defaultMode": "acceptEdits"
  }
}
```

### Model & Effort
```json
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "effortLevel": "high",
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### Environment Variables
```json
{
  "env": {
    "CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY": "1",
    "MY_CUSTOM_VAR": "value"
  }
}
```

### Common Settings
- `autoMemoryEnabled` - Enable auto memory (default: true)
- `editorMode` - "normal" or "vim"
- `language` - Response language (e.g., "japanese")
- `outputStyle` - Adjust system prompt style
- `tui` - "fullscreen" or "default" renderer
- `permissions` - Allow/deny/ask tool rules

## Managed Settings (Admin)

Deployed by IT through:
- Server (Claude.ai admin console)
- MDM/OS policies (macOS plist, Windows registry)
- File-based: `/etc/claude-code/` or `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\`

Managed settings **cannot be overridden** and support enforcement-only features like:
- `allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly` - Lock permission rules
- `allowManagedHooksOnly` - Only managed/SDK hooks run
- `allowManagedMcpServersOnly` - Restrict MCP servers

## Access Configuration

Run `/config` in Claude Code to open the tabbed Settings interface for visual configuration.

content.text:
Perfect. Based on the official Claude Code documentation:

**No CLI flags exist for thinking/effort control.** Non-interactive invocation (`claude -p "prompt"`) does not support flags like `--thinking`, `--effort`, or `--budget`.

However, **settings.json supports:**
- `effortLevel` — values: "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh" (affects reasoning depth)
- `alwaysThinkingEnabled` — boolean (enables extended thinking by default)
- `model` — select the model tier (opus vs. sonnet vs. haiku)

These settings live in `~/.claude/settings.json` (user-wide) or `.claude/settings.json` (project-specific) and apply to all invocations, including non-interactive ones. Precedence: local settings > project settings > user settings.

**No environment variables** control thinking budget directly. Settings files are the sole mechanism for persistent effort/thinking configuration.
